title: Block (and unblock) system updates
requirements: A Wii U already running the latest Aroma beta
warnings: A system update can patch the exploits your CFW relies on. Blocking updates keeps your setup working.
_note: Transcribed from wiiu.hacks.guide/block-updates and /unblock-updates. Everything here happens on the console, so no SD card selection is needed.

steps.title: Before you start
steps.body: Aroma's PayloadLoader already blocks updates on its own, but deleting the update folder adds extra protection against future patches. This all happens on the Wii U — make sure you have the **latest Aroma beta**.

steps.title: Block updates
steps.body:
Boot the Wii U into **Aroma**.

- If a **yellow warning screen** appears saying an update folder exists, press **X** to block updates.
- If no warning appears, hold **+ (Plus)** and **- (Minus)** together until the AutobootMenu confirms **"Updates are blocked"**.

That's it — updates are now blocked.

steps.title: Unblock updates (optional)
steps.body:
Only do this if you actually want to take a system update.

1. Boot Aroma while holding **+ (Plus)**.
2. Keep holding **+ (Plus)** and **- (Minus)** until it says **"Updates not blocked!"**.

If you also set Aroma to autoboot, you'll need to switch the boot title back to the Wii U Menu (via the PayloadLoader Installer → Check → Boot options → Switch back to Wii U Menu) before updating, then re-enable autoboot afterwards. Also set your DNS back to **Auto-obtain** under `Internet > Connect to the Internet > Connection List > your WiFi > Change Settings > DNS` if you had DNS blocks set.

steps.title: You're done
steps.body: Your Wii U is set the way you want. Leaving updates blocked is the safe default for keeping CFW working.
